tEn: Universal Manipulation Interface: In-The-Wild Robot Teaching Without In-The-Wild Robots
tZh: 通用操控接口：无需野外机器人的野外机器人教学
sEn: UMI enables zero-shot transfer of human manipulation skills to robots via a portable hand-held gripper and careful interface design.
sZh: UMI通过便携式手持夹持器和精心设计的接口，实现了从人类演示到机器人策略的零样本技能迁移。
absEn: We present Universal Manipulation Interface (UMI) -- a data collection and policy learning framework that allows direct skill transfer from in-the-wild human demonstrations to deployable robot policies. UMI employs hand-held grippers coupled with careful interface design to enable portable, low-cost, and information-rich data collection for challenging bimanual and dynamic manipulation demonstrations. To facilitate deployable policy learning, UMI incorporates a carefully designed policy interface with inference-time latency matching and a relative-trajectory action representation. The resulting learned policies are hardware-agnostic and deployable across multiple robot platforms. Equipped with these features, UMI framework unlocks new robot manipulation capabilities, allowing zero-shot generalizable dynamic, bimanual, precise, and long-horizon behaviors, by only changing the training data for each task. We demonstrate UMI's versatility and efficacy with comprehensive real-world experiments, where policies learned via UMI zero-shot generalize to novel environments and objects when trained on diverse human demonstrations. UMI's hardware and software system is open-sourced at https://umi-gripper.github.io.
absZh: 我们提出了通用操控接口（UMI）——一个数据收集和策略学习框架，允许从野外人类演示直接迁移技能到可部署的机器人策略。UMI采用手持式夹持器，结合精心设计的接口，实现便携、低成本且信息丰富的数据收集，用于具有挑战性的双手和动态操控演示。为了促进可部署的策略学习，UMI包含一个精心设计的策略接口，具有推理时延迟匹配和相对轨迹动作表示。由此学习到的策略与硬件无关，可跨多个机器人平台部署。凭借这些特性，UMI框架解锁了新的机器人操控能力，允许零样本泛化的动态、双手、精确和长时域行为，只需为每个任务更改训练数据。我们通过全面的真实世界实验展示了UMI的多功能性和有效性，其中通过UMI学习的策略在多样化的人类演示上训练后，零样本泛化到新环境和物体。UMI的硬件和软件系统已在https://umi-gripper.github.io开源。

contrib.en: Proposes Universal Manipulation Interface (UMI), a framework enabling direct skill transfer from in-the-wild human demonstrations to deployable robot policies.
contrib.zh: 提出通用操作接口（UMI），一种从野外人类演示直接迁移技能到可部署机器人策略的框架。

contrib.en: Designs a hand-held gripper with fisheye lens, side mirrors, and IMU-aware tracking for portable, low-cost, and information-rich data collection.
contrib.zh: 设计手持夹爪，配备鱼眼镜头、侧镜和IMU感知追踪，实现便携、低成本且信息丰富的数据采集。

contrib.en: Introduces inference-time latency matching and relative-trajectory action representation for hardware-agnostic policy deployment.
contrib.zh: 引入推理时延迟匹配和相对轨迹动作表示，实现硬件无关的策略部署。

contrib.en: Demonstrates zero-shot generalization to novel environments and objects across dynamic, bimanual, precise, and long-horizon tasks.
contrib.zh: 在动态、双手、精确和长时域任务中展示了对新环境和物体的零样本泛化能力。

contrib.en: Achieves 71.7% success rate in out-of-distribution tests with diverse in-the-wild data, outperforming narrow-domain baselines.
contrib.zh: 使用多样化野外数据在分布外测试中达到71.7%成功率，优于窄域基线。

contrib.en: Open-sources hardware and software system, enabling reproducible and accessible robot learning.
contrib.zh: 开源硬件和软件系统，实现可复现且易获取的机器人学习。

limits.en: Relies on kinematic data filtering to ensure feasibility, limiting transfer from hardware-infeasible actions.
limits.zh: 依赖运动学数据过滤确保可行性，限制了从硬件不可行动作的迁移。

limits.en: SLAM-based action recovery requires sufficient texture in the environment, failing in texture-deficient scenes.
limits.zh: 基于SLAM的动作恢复需要环境中有足够纹理，在纹理缺失场景中失效。

limits.en: Data collection with UMI gripper is less efficient than human hand demonstration due to weight and reduced degrees of freedom.
limits.zh: UMI夹爪的数据采集效率低于人手演示，因其重量和自由度减少。

limits.en: Policy performance depends on careful latency calibration, which varies across hardware platforms.
limits.zh: 策略性能依赖于仔细的延迟校准，该校准因硬件平台而异。

limits.en: Generalization to novel objects and environments requires diverse in-the-wild data, which may be costly to collect.
limits.zh: 泛化到新物体和环境需要多样化的野外数据，采集成本可能较高。

items.en: We present Universal Manipulation Interface (UMI) – a data collection and policy learning framework that allows direct skill transfer from in-the-wild human demonstrations to deployable robot policies. UMI employs hand-held grippers coupled with careful interface design to enable portable, low-cost, and information-rich data collection for challenging bimanual and dynamic manipulation demonstrations. To facilitate deployable policy learning, UMI incorporates a carefully designed policy interface with inference-time latency matching and a relative-trajectory action representation. The resulting learned policies are hardware-agnostic and deployable across multiple robot platforms. Equipped with these features, UMI framework unlocks new robot manipulation capabilities, allowing zero-shot generalizable dynamic, bimanual, precise, and long-horizon behaviors, by only changing the training data for each task. We demonstrate UMI’s versatility and efficacy with comprehensive real-world experiments, where policies learned via UMI zero-shot generalize to novel environments and objects when trained on diverse human demonstrations. UMI’s hardware and software system is open-sourced at https://umi-gripper.github.io.
items.zh: 我们提出了通用操作接口（UMI）——一个数据收集和策略学习框架，允许从野外人类演示直接迁移技能到可部署的机器人策略。UMI 使用手持式夹爪，结合精心设计的接口，实现便携、低成本且信息丰富的数据收集，用于具有挑战性的双手和动态操作演示。为了促进可部署的策略学习，UMI 包含一个精心设计的策略接口，具有推理时延迟匹配和相对轨迹动作表示。最终学习到的策略与硬件无关，可部署在多个机器人平台上。凭借这些特性，UMI 框架解锁了新的机器人操作能力，允许零样本泛化的动态、双手、精确和长时域行为，只需为每个任务更改训练数据。我们通过全面的真实世界实验展示了 UMI 的多功能性和有效性，其中通过 UMI 学习的策略在多样化的人类演示上训练后，零样本泛化到新环境和物体。UMI 的硬件和软件系统已在 https://umi-gripper.github.io 开源。

items.en: How should we demonstrate complex manipulation skills for robots to learn from? Attempts in the field have approached this question primarily from two directions: collecting targeted in-the-lab robot datasets via teleoperation or leveraging unstructured in-the-wild human videos. Unfortunately, neither is sufficient, as teleoperation requires high setup costs for hardware and expert operators, while human videos exhibit a large embodiment gap to robots.
items.zh: 我们应如何展示机器人学习的复杂操作技能？该领域的尝试主要从两个方向解决这个问题：通过遥操作收集针对性的实验室机器人数据集，或利用非结构化的野外人类视频。不幸的是，两者都不足够，因为遥操作需要高昂的硬件设置成本和专业操作员，而人类视频与机器人之间存在巨大的具身差距。

items.en: Recently, using sensorized hand-held grippers as a data collection interface [41, 50, 36] has emerged as a promising middle-ground alternative – simultaneously minimizing the embodiment gap while remaining intuitive and flexible. Despite their potential, these approaches still struggle to balance action diversity with transferability. While users can theoretically collect any actions with these hand-held devices, much of that data can not be transferred to an effective robot policy. As a result, despite achieving impressive visual diversity across hundreds of environments, the collected actions are constrained to simple grasping [41] or quasi-static pick-and-place [50, 36], lacking action diversity.
items.zh: 最近，使用传感器化的手持式夹爪作为数据收集接口 [41, 50, 36] 已成为一个有前景的折中方案——同时最小化具身差距，同时保持直观和灵活。尽管有潜力，这些方法仍然难以平衡动作多样性与可迁移性。虽然用户理论上可以用这些手持设备收集任何动作，但其中许多数据无法迁移到有效的机器人策略。因此，尽管在数百个环境中实现了令人印象深刻的视觉多样性，收集的动作仅限于简单抓取 [41] 或准静态拾取和放置 [50, 36]，缺乏动作多样性。

items.capEn: Figure 2: UMI Demonstration Interface Design. Left: Hand-held grippers for data collection, with a GoPro as the only sensor and recording device. Middle: Image from the GoPro’s 155° Fisheye view. Note the physical side mirrors highlighted in green which provide implicit stereo information. Right: UMI-compatible robot gripper and camera setup make observation similar to hand-held gripper view.
items.capZh: 图 2：UMI 演示接口设计。左：用于数据收集的手持式夹爪，仅使用 GoPro 作为传感器和记录设备。中：GoPro 155° 鱼眼视图的图像。注意以绿色高亮显示的物理侧镜，提供隐式立体信息。右：UMI 兼容的机器人夹爪和相机设置，使观察与手持式夹爪视图相似。

items.en: What prevents action transfer in previous work? We identified a few subtle yet critical issues:
items.zh: 是什么阻止了先前工作中的动作迁移？我们识别出几个微妙但关键的问题：

items.en: Insufficient visual context: While using a wrist-mounted camera is key for aligning the observation space and enhancing device portability, it restricts the scene’s visual coverage. The camera’s proximity to the manipulated object often results in heavy occlusions, providing insufficient visual context for action planning.
items.zh: 视觉上下文不足：虽然使用腕戴式相机对于对齐观察空间和增强设备便携性至关重要，但它限制了场景的视觉覆盖。相机靠近操作对象通常导致严重遮挡，为动作规划提供的视觉上下文不足。

items.en: Action imprecision: Most hand-held devices rely on monocular structure-from-motion (SfM) to recover robot actions. However, such methods often struggle to recover precise global action due to scale ambiguity, motion blur, or insufficient texture, which significantly restrict the precision of tasks for which the system can be employed.
items.zh: 动作不精确：大多数手持设备依赖单目运动恢复结构（SfM）来恢复机器人动作。然而，此类方法通常因尺度模糊、运动模糊或纹理不足而难以恢复精确的全局动作，这显著限制了系统可执行任务的精度。

items.en: Latency discrepancies: During hand-held data collection, observation and action recording occur without latency. However, during inference, various latency sources, including sensor, inference, and execution latencies, arise within the system. Policies unaware of these latency discrepancies will encounter out-of-distribution input and in turn, generate out-of-sync actions. This issue is especially salient for fast and dynamic actions.
items.zh: 延迟差异：在手持数据收集期间，观察和动作记录无延迟发生。然而，在推理期间，系统内会出现各种延迟源，包括传感器、推理和执行延迟。未意识到这些延迟差异的策略将遇到分布外输入，进而生成不同步的动作。此问题对于快速和动态动作尤为突出。

items.en: Insufficient policy representation: Prior works often use simple policy representations (e.g., MLPs) with action regression loss, limiting their capacity to capture complex multimodal action distributions inherent in human data. Consequently, even with precisely recovered demonstrated actions and all discrepancies removed, the resulting policy could still struggle to fit the data accurately. This further hampers large-scale, distributed human data collection, as more demonstrators increase action multimodality.
items.zh: 策略表示不足：先前工作通常使用简单的策略表示（例如 MLP）和动作回归损失，限制了它们捕捉人类数据中固有的复杂多模态动作分布的能力。因此，即使精确恢复了演示动作并消除了所有差异，所得策略仍可能难以准确拟合数据。这进一步阻碍了大规模、分布式的人类数据收集，因为更多演示者增加了动作多模态性。

items.en: In this paper, we address these issues with careful design of the demonstration and policy interface:
items.zh: 在本文中，我们通过精心设计演示和策略接口来解决这些问题：

items.en: First, we aim to identify the right physical interface for human demonstration that is intuitive and meanwhile able to capture all the information necessary for policy learning. Specifically, we use a Fisheye lens to increase the field of view and visual context, and add side mirrors on the gripper to provide implicit stereo observation. When combined with the GoPro’s built-in IMU sensor, we can enable robust tracking under fast motion.
items.zh: 首先，我们旨在确定正确的人类演示物理接口，该接口直观且能捕捉策略学习所需的所有信息。具体来说，我们使用鱼眼镜头增加视野和视觉上下文，并在夹爪上添加侧镜以提供隐式立体观察。当与 GoPro 内置的 IMU 传感器结合时，我们可以在快速运动下实现鲁棒跟踪。

items.en: Second, we explore the right policy interface (i.e., observation and action representations) that could make the policy hardware-agnostic and thereby enable effective skill transfer. Concretely, we employ inference-time latency matching to handle different sensor observation and execution latency, use relative trajectory as action representation to remove the need for precise global action, and finally, apply Diffusion Policy [9] to model multimodal action distributions.
items.zh: 其次，我们探索正确的策略接口（即观察和动作表示），使策略与硬件无关，从而促进有效的技能迁移。具体来说，我们采用推理时延迟匹配来处理不同的传感器观察和执行延迟，使用相对轨迹作为动作表示以消除对精确全局动作的需求，最后应用扩散策略 [9] 来建模多模态动作分布。

items.en: The final system, Universal Manipulation Interface (UMI), provides a practical and accessible framework to unlock new robot manipulation skills, allowing us to demonstrate any actions in any environment while maintaining high transferability from human demonstration to robot policy.
items.zh: 最终系统，通用操作接口（UMI），提供了一个实用且可访问的框架，以解锁新的机器人操作技能，允许我们在任何环境中演示任何动作，同时保持从人类演示到机器人策略的高可迁移性。

items.en: With just a wrist-mounted camera on the hand-held gripper (Fig. 2), we show that UMI is capable of achieving a wide range of manipulation tasks that involve dynamic, bimanual, precise and long-horizon actions by only changing the training data for each task (Fig. 1). Furthermore, when trained with diverse human demonstrations, the final policy exhibits zero-shot generalization to novel environments and objects, achieving a remarkable 70%percent7070\% success rate in out-of-distribution tests, a level of generalizabilty seldomly observed in other behavior cloning frameworks. We open-source the hardware and software system at https://umi-gripper.github.io.
items.zh: 仅凭手持式夹爪上的腕戴式相机（图 2），我们展示了 UMI 能够实现涉及动态、双手、精确和长时域动作的广泛操作任务，只需为每个任务更改训练数据（图 1）。此外，当使用多样化的人类演示进行训练时，最终策略展现出对新环境和物体的零样本泛化能力，在分布外测试中实现了 70% 的显著成功率，这种泛化水平在其他行为克隆框架中很少见。我们在 https://umi-gripper.github.io 开源了硬件和软件系统。

items.en: A key enabler for any data-driven robotics system is the data itself. Here, we review a few typical data collection workflows in the context of robotic manipulation.
items.zh: 任何数据驱动机器人系统的关键推动因素是数据本身。这里，我们回顾机器人操作背景下几种典型的数据收集工作流程。

full.secZh: 二-A 遥操作机器人数据

items.en: Imitation learning learns policies from expert demonstrations. Behavior cloning (BC), utilizing teleoperated robot demonstrations, stands out for its direct transferability. However, teleoperating real robots for data collection poses significant challenges. Previous approaches utilized interfaces such as 3D spacemouse [9, 54], VR or AR controllers [35, 3, 13, 19, 31, 51, 12], smartphones [44, 45, 22], and haptic devices [38, 47, 43, 26, 4] for teleoperation. These methods are either very expensive or hard to use due to high latency and lack of user intuitiveness. While recent advancements in leader-follower (i.e. puppetting) devices such as ALOHA [53, 15] and GELLO [46] offer promise with intuitive and low-cost interfaces, their reliance on real robots during data collection limits the type and number of environments the system can gain access to for “in-the-wild” data acquisition. Exoskeletons [14, 20] remove the dependence on real robots during data collection, however, require fine-tuning using teleoperated real robot data for deployment. Moreover, the resulting data and policy from aforementioned devices are embodiment-specific, preventing reusage for different robots.
items.zh: 模仿学习从专家演示中学习策略。行为克隆（BC）利用遥操作的机器人演示，因其直接可迁移性而突出。然而，遥操作真实机器人进行数据收集面临重大挑战。先前的方法使用诸如 3D 空间鼠标 [9, 54]、VR 或 AR 控制器 [35, 3, 13, 19, 31, 51, 12]、智能手机 [44, 45, 22] 和触觉设备 [38, 47, 43, 26, 4] 等接口进行遥操作。这些方法要么非常昂贵，要么由于高延迟和缺乏用户直观性而难以使用。虽然最近在领导者-跟随者（即木偶）设备方面的进展，如 ALOHA [53, 15] 和 GELLO [46]，提供了直观且低成本的接口，但它们在数据收集期间依赖真实机器人，限制了系统可访问的环境类型和数量，以进行“野外”数据采集。外骨骼 [14, 20] 消除了数据收集期间对真实机器人的依赖，但需要在使用遥操作的真实机器人数据进行微调后才能部署。此外，上述设备产生的数据和策略是具身特定的，阻止了在不同机器人上的重用。

items.en: In contrast, UMI eliminates the need for physical robots during data collection and offers a more portable interface for in-the-wild robot teaching, providing data and policies that are transferable to different robot embodiments (e.g., 6DoF or 7DoF robot arms).
items.zh: 相比之下，UMI 消除了数据收集期间对物理机器人的需求，并为野外机器人教学提供了更便携的接口，提供可迁移到不同机器人具身（例如 6 自由度或 7 自由度机器人臂）的数据和策略。

full.sec: II-B Visual Demonstrations from Human Video
full.secZh: 二-B 人类视频视觉演示

items.en: There’s a distinct line of work dedicated to policy learning from in-the-wild video data (e.g. YouTube videos). The most common way is to learn from diverse passive human demonstration videos. Utilizing passive human demonstrations, previous works learn task cost functions [37, 8, 1, 21], affordance functions [2], dense object descriptors [40, 24, 39], action correspondences [33, 28], and pre-trained visual representations [23, 48].
items.zh: 有一条独立的工作线致力于从野外视频数据（例如 YouTube 视频）学习策略。最常见的方法是从多样化的被动人类演示视频中学习。利用被动人类演示，先前的工作学习任务成本函数 [37, 8, 1, 21]、可供性函数 [2]、密集对象描述符 [40, 24, 39]、动作对应关系 [33, 28] 和预训练的视觉表示 [23, 48]。

items.en: However, this approach encounters three major challenges. Firstly, most video demonstrations lack explicit action information, crucial for learning generalizable policies. To infer action data from passive human video, previous works resort to hand pose detectors [44, 1, 38, 28], or combining human videos with in-domain teleoperated robot data to predict actions [33, 20, 34, 28]. Second, the evident embodiment gap between humans and robots hinders action transfer. Efforts to bridge the gap include learning human-to-robot action mapping with hand pose retargetting [38, 28] or extracting embodiment-agnostic keypoints [49]. Despite these attempts, the inherent embodiment differences still complicate policy transfer from human video to physical robots. Thirdly, the inherent observation gap induced by the embodiment gap in this line of work introduces inevitable mismatch between train/inference time observation data, exacerbating the transferability of the resulting policies, despite efforts in aligning demonstration observation with robot observation [20, 28].
items.zh: 然而，这种方法遇到三个主要挑战。首先，大多数视频演示缺乏明确的动作信息，这对于学习可泛化的策略至关重要。为了从被动人类视频推断动作数据，先前的工作依赖于手部姿态检测器 [44, 1, 38, 28]，或将人类视频与领域内遥操作的机器人数据结合以预测动作 [33, 20, 34, 28]。其次，人类与机器人之间明显的具身差距阻碍了动作迁移。弥合这一差距的努力包括使用手部姿态重定向学习人类到机器人的动作映射 [38, 28]，或提取与具身无关的关键点 [49]。尽管有这些尝试，固有的具身差异仍然使策略从人类视频迁移到物理机器人变得复杂。第三，这一工作线中由具身差距引起的固有观察差距，在训练/推理时间观察数据之间引入了不可避免的不匹配，尽管在将演示观察与机器人观察对齐方面做出了努力 [20, 28]，但加剧了所得策略的可迁移性。

items.en: In contrast, data collected with UMI exhibit minimal embodiment gap both in action and observation spaces, enabled by precise manipulation action extraction via robust visual-inertial camera tracking and the shared Fisheye wrist-mounted cameras during teaching and testing. Consequently, this enables in-the-wild zero-shot policy transfer for dynamic, bimanual, precise, and long-horizon manipulation tasks.
items.zh: 相比之下，使用 UMI 收集的数据在动作和观察空间中均表现出最小的具身差距，这得益于通过鲁棒的视觉-惯性相机跟踪实现的精确操作动作提取，以及在教学和测试期间共享的鱼眼腕戴式相机。因此，这实现了动态、双手、精确和长时域操作任务的野外零样本策略迁移。

full.sec: II-C Hand-Held Grippers for Quasi-static Actions
full.secZh: 二-C 手持夹爪准静态动作

items.en: Hand-held grippers [41, 50, 10, 32, 27, 25] minimize observation embodiment gaps in manipulation data collection, offering portability and intuitive interfaces for efficient data collection in the wild. However, accurately and robustly extracting 6DoF end-effector (EE) pose from these devices remains challenging, hindering the deployment of robot policies learned from these data on fine-grained manipulation tasks.
items.zh: 手持式夹爪[41, 50, 10, 32, 27, 25]通过提供便携性和直观界面，最小化了操作数据收集中的观察具身差距，从而实现在野外高效收集数据。然而，从这些设备中准确且鲁棒地提取6自由度末端执行器（EE）位姿仍然具有挑战性，阻碍了从这些数据中学习的机器人策略在精细操作任务中的部署。

items.en: Prior works attempted to address this issue through various approaches, such as SfM [50, 25] which suffers from scale ambiguity; RGB-D fusion [41] which requires expensive sensors and onboard compute; external motion tracking [32, 27] which is limited to lab settings. These devices, constrained to quasi-static actions due to low EE tracking accuracy and robustness, often necessitate cumbersome onboard computer or external motion capture (MoCap) systems, diminishing their feasibility for in-the-wild data collection. In contrast, UMI integrates state-of-the-art SLAM [6] with built-in IMU data from GoPro, to accurately capture 6DoF actions at the global scale. The high-accuracy data enables trained BC policy to learn bimanual tasks. With thorough latency matching, UMI further enables real-world deployable policy for dynamic actions such as tossing.
items.zh: 先前的工作尝试通过多种方法解决这一问题，例如存在尺度模糊性的SfM[50, 25]；需要昂贵传感器和机载算力的RGB-D融合[41]；以及局限于实验室环境的外部运动跟踪[32, 27]。由于末端执行器跟踪精度和鲁棒性较低，这些设备被限制在准静态动作中，通常需要笨重的机载计算机或外部运动捕捉（MoCap）系统，降低了其在野外数据收集中的可行性。相比之下，UMI将最先进的SLAM[6]与GoPro内置的IMU数据相结合，以全局尺度精确捕获6自由度动作。高精度数据使得训练的行为克隆策略能够学习双手任务。通过彻底的延迟匹配，UMI进一步实现了可部署于现实世界的策略，用于诸如抛掷等动态动作。

items.en: Recently, Dobb-E [36] proposed a “reacher-grabber” tool mounted with an iPhone for pick-and-place demonstrations. Yet, Dobb-E only demonstrates policy deployment for quasi-static tasks and requires environment-specific policy fine-tuning. Conversely, using only data collected with UMI enables trained policy to zero-shot generalize to novel in-the-wild environments, unseen objects, multiple robot embodiments, for dynamic, bimanual, precise and long-horizon tasks.
items.zh: 最近，Dobb-E[36]提出了一种安装有iPhone的“拾取器-抓取器”工具，用于拾放演示。然而，Dobb-E仅展示了准静态任务的策略部署，并且需要针对特定环境的策略微调。相比之下，仅使用UMI收集的数据即可使训练的策略零样本泛化到新的野外环境、未见过的物体、多种机器人具身，以及动态、双手、精确和长时域任务。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Fisheye vs Rectilinear (a) UMI policies use raw Fisheye image as observation. (b) Rectifying a large 155° FoV image to the pin-hole model severely stretches the peripheral view (outside of blue line), while compresses the most important information at the center to a small area (inside of red line).
items.capZh: 图3：鱼眼与直线透视 (a) UMI策略使用原始鱼眼图像作为观察。(b) 将155°大视场图像校正为针孔模型会严重拉伸周边视野（蓝线外），同时将最重要的中心信息压缩到小区域（红线内）。

items.en: Universal Manipulation Interface (UMI) is hand-held data collection and policy learning framework that allows direct transfer from in-the-wild human demonstrations to deployable robot policies. It is designed with the following goals in mind:
items.zh: 通用操作接口（UMI）是一种手持式数据收集和策略学习框架，允许从野外人类演示直接迁移到可部署的机器人策略。其设计目标如下：

items.en: Portable. The hand-held UMI grippers can be taken to any environment and start data collection with close-to-zero setup time.
items.zh: 便携性。手持式UMI夹爪可被带到任何环境，并以接近零的设置时间开始数据收集。

items.en: Capable. The ability to capture and transfer natural and complex human manipulation skills beyond pick-and-place.
items.zh: 能力。能够捕获和迁移超越拾放的自然复杂人类操作技能。

items.en: Sufficient. The collected data should contain sufficient information for learning effective robot policies and contain minimal embodiment-specific information that would prevent transfer.
items.zh: 充分性。收集的数据应包含足够的信息以学习有效的机器人策略，并包含最少的阻碍迁移的具身特定信息。

items.en: Reproducible: Researchers and enthusiasts should be able to consistently build UMI grippers and use data to train their own robots, even with different robot arms.
items.zh: 可复现性：研究人员和爱好者应能够一致地构建UMI夹爪，并使用数据训练自己的机器人，即使使用不同的机器人手臂。

items.en: The following sections describe how we enable the above goals through our hardware and policy interface design.
items.zh: 以下章节描述了我们如何通过硬件和策略接口设计实现上述目标。

items.en: UMI’s data collection hardware takes the form of a trigger-activated, handheld 3D printed parallel jaw gripper with soft fingers, mounted with a GoPro camera as the only sensor and recording device (see HD1). For bimanual manipulation, UMI can be trivially extended with another gripper. The key research question we need to address here is:
items.zh: UMI的数据收集硬件采用触发式手持3D打印平行爪夹爪，配有软指，并安装GoPro相机作为唯一传感器和记录设备（见HD1）。对于双手操作，UMI可以轻松扩展另一个夹爪。我们需要解决的关键研究问题是：

items.en: Specifically, on the observation side, the device needs to capture sufficient visual context to infer action HD2 and critical depth information HD3. On the action side, it needs to capture precise robot action under fast human motion HD4, detailed subtle adjustments on griping width HD5, and automatically check whether each demonstration is valid given the robot hardware kinematics HD6. The following sections describe details on how we achieve these goals.
items.zh: 具体来说，在观察方面，设备需要捕获足够的视觉上下文以推断动作HD2和关键深度信息HD3。在动作方面，它需要在快速人类运动下捕获精确的机器人动作HD4、抓取宽度的详细细微调整HD5，并自动检查每个演示在给定机器人硬件运动学下是否有效HD6。以下章节详细描述我们如何实现这些目标。

items.en: HD1. Wrist-mounted cameras as input observation. We rely solely on wrist-mounted cameras, without the need for any external camera setups. When deploying UMI on a robot, we place GoPro cameras with the same location with respect to the same 3D-printed fingers as on the hand-held gripper. This design provides the following benefits:
items.zh: HD1. 腕装相机作为输入观察。我们仅依赖腕装相机，无需任何外部相机设置。在机器人上部署UMI时，我们将GoPro相机放置在与手持夹爪上相同的3D打印手指相对位置。这种设计提供了以下优势：

items.en: Minimizing the observation embodiment gaps. Thanks to our hardware design, the videos observed in wrist-mount cameras are almost indistinguishable between human demonstrations and robot deployment, making the policy input less sensitive to embodiment.
items.zh: 最小化观察具身差距。得益于我们的硬件设计，腕装相机观察到的视频在人类演示和机器人部署之间几乎无法区分，使得策略输入对具身不那么敏感。

items.en: Mechanical robustness. Because the camera is mechanically fixed relative to the fingers, mounting UMI on robots does not require camera-robot-world calibration. Hence, the system is much more robust to mechanical shocks, making it easy to deploy.
items.zh: 机械鲁棒性。由于相机相对于手指机械固定，将UMI安装在机器人上不需要相机-机器人-世界标定。因此，系统对机械冲击更加鲁棒，易于部署。

items.en: Portable hardware setup. Without the need for an external static camera or additional onboard compute, we largely simplify the data collection setup and make the whole system highly portable.
items.zh: 便携硬件设置。无需外部静态相机或额外机载算力，我们大大简化了数据收集设置，使整个系统高度便携。

items.en: Camera motion for natural data diversification. A side benefit we observed from experiments is that when training with a moving camera, the policy learns to focus on task-relevant objects or regions instead of background structures (similar in effect to random cropping). As a result, the final policy naturally becomes more robust against distractors at inference time.
items.zh: 相机运动实现自然数据多样化。我们从实验中观察到的附带好处是，当使用移动相机训练时，策略学会关注任务相关物体或区域，而非背景结构（类似于随机裁剪的效果）。因此，最终策略在推理时自然对干扰物更加鲁棒。

items.en: Avoiding use of external static cameras also introduce additional challenges for for downstream policy learning. For example, the policy now needs to handle non-stationary and partial observations. We mitigated these issues by leveraging wide-FoV Fisheye Lens HD2, and robust visual tracking HD4, described in the following sections.
items.zh: 避免使用外部静态相机也为下游策略学习带来了额外挑战。例如，策略现在需要处理非平稳和部分观察。我们通过利用大视场鱼眼镜头HD2和鲁棒视觉跟踪HD4来缓解这些问题，详见以下章节。

items.en: HD2. Fisheye Lens for visual context. We use a 155-degree Fisheye lens attachment on wrist-mounted GoPro camera, which provides sufficient visual context for a wide range of tasks, as shown in Fig. 2. As the policy input, we directly use raw Fisheye images without undistortion since Fisheye effects conveniently preserve resolution in the center while compressing information in the peripheral view. In contrast, rectified pinhole image (Fig. 3 right) exhibits extreme distortions, making it unsuitable for learning due to the wide FoV. Beyond improving SLAM robustness with increased visual features and overlap [52], our quantitative evaluation (Sec V-A) shows that the Fisheye lens improves policy performance by providing the necessary visual context.
items.zh: HD2. 用于视觉上下文的鱼眼镜头。我们在腕装GoPro相机上使用155度鱼眼镜头附件，为广泛任务提供足够的视觉上下文，如图2所示。作为策略输入，我们直接使用原始鱼眼图像而不进行去畸变，因为鱼眼效应方便地保留了中心分辨率，同时压缩了周边视野信息。相比之下，校正后的针孔图像（图3右）表现出极端畸变，由于大视场而不适合学习。除了通过增加视觉特征和重叠来提高SLAM鲁棒性[52]外，我们的定量评估（第V-A节）表明，鱼眼镜头通过提供必要的视觉上下文提高了策略性能。

items.capEn: Figure 4: UMI Side Mirrors. The ultra-wide-angle camera coupled with strategically positioned mirrors, facilitates implicit stereo depth estimation. (a): The view through each mirror effectively creates two virtual cameras, whose poses are reflected along the mirror planes with respect to the main camera. (b): Ketchup on the plate, occluded from the main camera view, is visible inside the right mirror, proving that mirrors simulate cameras with different optical centers. (c): We digitally reflect the content inside mirrors for policy observation. Note the orientation of the cup handle becomes consistent across all 3 views after reflection.
items.capZh: 图4：UMI侧镜。超广角相机与策略定位的镜子相结合，有助于隐式立体深度估计。(a)：每个镜子的视野有效地创建了两个虚拟相机，其位姿相对于主相机沿镜面反射。(b)：盘子上的番茄酱，在主相机视野中被遮挡，但在右镜中可见，证明镜子模拟了具有不同光心的相机。(c)：我们对镜子内的内容进行数字反射，用于策略观察。注意反射后杯把手的朝向在所有三个视图中变得一致。

items.capEn: Figure 5: UMI Policy Interface Design. (b) UMI policy takes in a sequence of synchronized observations (RGB image, relative EE pose, and gripper width) and outputs a sequence of desired relative EE pose and gripper width as action. (a) We synchronize different observation streams with physically measured latencies. (c) We send action commands ahead of time to compensate for robots’ execution latency.
items.capZh: 图5：UMI策略接口设计。(b) UMI策略接收一系列同步观测（RGB图像、相对末端执行器位姿和夹爪宽度），并输出一系列期望的相对末端执行器位姿和夹爪宽度作为动作。(a) 我们通过物理测量的延迟来同步不同的观测流。(c) 我们提前发送动作指令以补偿机器人的执行延迟。

items.en: HD3. Side mirrors for implicit stereo. To mitigate the lack of direct depth perception from the monocular camera view, we placed a pair of physical mirrors in the cameras’ peripheral view which creates implicit stereo views all in the same image. As illustrated in Fig 4 (a), the images inside the mirrors are equivalent to what can be seen from additional cameras reflected along the mirror plane, without the additional cost and weight. To make use of these mirror views, we found that digitally reflecting the crop of the images in the mirrors, shown in Fig 4 (c), yields the best result for policy learning (Sec. V-A). Note that without digital reflection, the orientation of objects seen through side mirrors is the opposite of that in the main camera view.
items.zh: HD3. 用于隐式立体视觉的侧镜。为缓解单目相机视角缺乏直接深度感知的问题，我们在相机周边视野放置了一对物理镜子，从而在同一图像中创建隐式立体视图。如图4(a)所示，镜子内的图像等效于沿镜面反射的附加相机所看到的图像，且无需额外成本和重量。为利用这些镜像视图，我们发现对镜子中的图像区域进行数字反射（如图4(c)所示）可获得最佳策略学习效果（见第V-A节）。注意，若不进行数字反射，通过侧镜看到的物体方向与主相机视图中的方向相反。

items.en: HD4. IMU-aware tracking. UMI captures rapid movements with absolute scale by leveraging GoPro’s built-in capability to record IMU data (accelerometer and gyroscope) into standard mp4 video files [18]. By jointly optimizing visual tracking and inertial pose constraints, our Inertial-monocular SLAM system based on OBR-SLAM3 [7] maintains tracking for a short period of time even if visual tracking fails due to motion blur or a lack of visual features (e.g. looking down at a table). This allows UMI to capture and deploy highly dynamic actions such as tossing (shown in Fig 7). In addition, the joint visual-inertial optimization allows direct recovery of real metric scale, important for action precision and inter-gripper pose proprioception PD2.3: a critical ingredient to enable bimanual policy.
items.zh: HD4. 惯性测量单元感知跟踪。UMI利用GoPro内置的将IMU数据（加速度计和陀螺仪）记录到标准mp4视频文件的功能[18]，以绝对尺度捕捉快速运动。通过联合优化视觉跟踪和惯性位姿约束，我们的基于OBR-SLAM3[7]的惯性-单目SLAM系统即使在视觉跟踪因运动模糊或缺乏视觉特征（如向下看桌子）而失败时，也能在短时间内保持跟踪。这使得UMI能够捕捉和部署高度动态的动作，如抛掷（如图7所示）。此外，联合视觉-惯性优化允许直接恢复真实公制尺度，这对动作精度和夹爪间位姿本体感觉PD2.3至关重要：这是实现双臂策略的关键要素。

items.en: HD5. Continuous gripper control. In contrast to the binary open-close action used in prior works [41, 44, 54], we found commanding gripper width continuously significantly expands the range of tasks doable by parallel-jaw grippers. For example, the tossing task (Fig. 7) requires precise timing for releasing objects. Since objects have different widths, binary gripper actions will be unlikely to meet the precision requirement. On UMI gripper, finger width is continuously tracked via fiducial markers [16] (Fig. 2 left). Using series-elastic end effectors principle [42], UMI can implicitly record and control grasp forces by regulating the deformation of soft fingers through continuous gripper width control.
items.zh: HD5. 连续夹爪控制。与先前工作中使用的二元开合动作[41, 44, 54]相比，我们发现连续控制夹爪宽度可显著扩展平行颚式夹爪可完成的任务范围。例如，抛掷任务（图7）需要精确的释放时机。由于物体宽度不同，二元夹爪动作难以满足精度要求。在UMI夹爪上，手指宽度通过基准标记[16]连续跟踪（图2左）。利用串联弹性末端执行器原理[42]，UMI可通过连续夹爪宽度控制调节软手指的变形，从而隐式记录和控制抓取力。

items.en: HD6. Kinematic-based data filtering. While the data collection process is robot-agnostic, we apply simple kinematic-based data filtering to select valid trajectories for different robot embodiments. Concretely, when the robot’s base location and kinematics are known, the absolute end-effector pose recovered by SLAM allows kinematics and dynamics feasibility filtering on the demonstration data. Training on the filtered dataset ensures policies comply with embodiment-specific kinematic constraints.
items.zh: HD6. 基于运动学的数据过滤。虽然数据收集过程与机器人无关，但我们应用简单的基于运动学的数据过滤来为不同机器人实体选择有效轨迹。具体而言，当机器人基座位置和运动学已知时，SLAM恢复的绝对末端执行器位姿允许对演示数据进行运动学和动力学可行性过滤。在过滤后的数据集上训练可确保策略符合特定实体的运动学约束。

items.en: Putting everything together. The UMI gripper weighs 780g, with an external dimension of L​310​m​m×W​175​m​m×H​210​m​m𝐿310𝑚𝑚𝑊175𝑚𝑚𝐻210𝑚𝑚L310mm\times W175mm\times H210mm and finger stroke of 80​m​m80𝑚𝑚80mm. The 3D printed gripper has a BoM cost of $73, while the GoPro camera and accessories total $298. As shown in Fig. 2, we can equip any robot arms with a compatible gripper and camera setup.
items.zh: 综合所有设计。UMI夹爪重780克，外部尺寸为L310mm×W175mm×H210mm，手指行程为80mm。3D打印夹爪的物料清单成本为73美元，而GoPro相机及配件总计298美元。如图2所示，我们可以为任何机器人手臂配备兼容的夹爪和相机设置。

items.en: With the collected demonstration data, we can train a visuomotor policy that takes in a sequence of synchronized observations (RGB images, 6 degrees-of-freedom end-effector pose, and gripper width) and produces a sequence of actions (end-effector pose and gripper width) as shown in Fig. 5 (b). In this paper, we use Diffusion Policy [9] for all of our experiments, while other frameworks such as ACT [53] could potentially serve as a drop-in replacement.
items.zh: 利用收集到的演示数据，我们可以训练一个视觉运动策略，该策略接收一系列同步观测（RGB图像、6自由度末端执行器位姿和夹爪宽度），并生成一系列动作（末端执行器位姿和夹爪宽度），如图5(b)所示。在本文中，我们使用扩散策略[9]进行所有实验，而其他框架如ACT[53]也可作为即插即用的替代方案。

items.en: An important goal of UMI’s policy interface design is to ensure the interface is agnostic to underlying robotic hardware platforms such that the resulting policy, trained on one data source (i.e., hand-held gripper), could be directly deployed to different robot platforms. To do so, we aim to address the following two key challenges:
items.zh: UMI策略接口设计的一个重要目标是确保接口与底层机器人硬件平台无关，使得在一种数据源（即手持夹爪）上训练的策略能够直接部署到不同的机器人平台上。为此，我们旨在解决以下两个关键挑战：

items.en: Hardware-specific latency. The latency of various hardware (streaming camera, robot controller, industrial gripper) is highly variable across system deployments, ranging from single-digit to hundreds of milliseconds. In contrast, all information streams captured by UMI grippers have zero latency with respect to the image observation, thanks to GoPro’s synchronized video, IMU measurements and the vision-based gripper width estimation.
items.zh: 硬件特定延迟。各种硬件（流媒体相机、机器人控制器、工业夹爪）的延迟在不同系统部署中差异很大，从个位数到数百毫秒不等。相比之下，UMI夹爪捕获的所有信息流相对于图像观测具有零延迟，这得益于GoPro的同步视频、IMU测量和基于视觉的夹爪宽度估计。

items.en: Embodiment-specific proprioception. Commonly used proprioception observations such as joint angles and EE pose are only well-defined with respect to a specific robot arm and robot base placement. In contrast, UMI needs to collect data across diverse environments and be generalizable to multiple robot embodiments.
items.zh: 实体特定本体感觉。常用的本体感觉观测（如关节角度和末端执行器位姿）仅相对于特定机器人手臂和机器人基座位置定义。相比之下，UMI需要在不同环境中收集数据，并能泛化到多种机器人实体。

items.en: In the following sections, we will describe three policy interface designs that address these challenges.
items.zh: 在以下章节中，我们将描述解决这些挑战的三个策略接口设计。

items.en: PD1. Inference-time latency matching. While UMI’s policy interface assumes synchronized observation streams and immediate action execution, physical robot systems do not conform to this assumption. If not carefully handled, the timing mismatch between training and testing can cause large performance drops on dynamic manipulation tasks that require rapid movement and precise hand-eye coordination, demonstrated in Sec V-B. In this paper, we separately handle timing discrepancies on the observation and action sides:
items.zh: PD1. 推理时延迟匹配。虽然UMI的策略接口假设同步观测流和即时动作执行，但物理机器人系统并不符合这一假设。若不仔细处理，训练和测试之间的时序不匹配会导致需要快速运动和精确手眼协调的动态操作任务性能大幅下降，如第V-B节所示。在本文中，我们分别处理观测侧和动作侧的时序差异：

items.en: PD1.1) Observation latency matching. On real robotic systems, different observation streams (RGB image, EE pose, gripper width) are captured by distributed micro-controllers, resulting in different observation latency.
items.zh: PD1.1) 观测延迟匹配。在真实机器人系统中，不同的观测流（RGB图像、末端执行器位姿、夹爪宽度）由分布式微控制器捕获，导致不同的观测延迟。对于每个观测流，我们单独测量其延迟（详见§A.0-A1至A.0-A3）。在推理时，我们将所有观测相对于延迟最高的流（通常是相机）对齐。具体而言，我们首先将RGB相机观测时间下采样到所需频率（通常为10-20Hz），然后使用每个图像的捕获时间戳t_obs对夹爪和机器人本体感觉流进行线性插值。在双臂系统中，我们通过寻找最近邻帧来软同步两个相机，最大偏差为1/60秒。结果得到一系列符合UMI策略的同步观测，如图5(a)所示。

items.en: For each observation stream, we individually measure their latency (details see §A.0-A1-A.0-A3). At inference time, we align all observations with respect to the stream with the highest latency (usually the camera). Specifically, we first temporally down-sample the RGB camera observations to the desired frequency (often 10-20Hz), and then use the capture timestamp of each image to​b​ssubscript𝑡𝑜𝑏𝑠t_{obs} to linearly interpolate gripper and robot proprioception streams. In bimanual systems, we soft-synchronize two cameras by finding the nearest neighbor frames, which can be off by a maximum of 160160\frac{1}{60} seconds. The result is a sequence of synchronized observations that conform to UMI policy, shown in Fig. 5 (a).
items.zh: PD1.2) 动作延迟匹配。UMI策略假设输出为一系列同步的末端执行器位姿和夹爪宽度。然而，在实践中，机器人手臂和夹爪只能以一定的执行延迟跟踪期望的位姿序列，该延迟因机器人硬件而异。为确保机器人和夹爪在期望时间（由策略给出）达到期望位姿，我们需要提前发送指令以补偿执行延迟，如图5(c)所示。执行延迟校准细节见§A.0-A4。

items.en: PD1.2) Action latency matching. UMI policy assumes the output as a sequence of synchronized EE poses and gripper widths. However, in practice, robot arms and grippers can only track the desired pose sequence up to an execution latency, that varies across different robot hardware. To make sure the robots and grippers reach the desired pose at the desired time (given by the policy), we need to send commands ahead of time to compensate for execution latency, as shown in Fig. 5 (c). See §A.0-A4 for execution latency calibration details.
items.zh: 在执行过程中，UMI策略预测从观测最后一步t_obs开始的动作序列。由于观测延迟t_input - t_obs、策略推理延迟t_output - t_input和执行延迟t_act - t_output，预测的前几个动作立即过时。我们简单地丢弃过时的动作，仅对每个硬件执行时间戳在t_act之后的期望动作。

items.en: During execution, the UMI policy predicts the action sequence starting at the last step of observation to​b​ssubscript𝑡𝑜𝑏𝑠t_{obs}. The first few actions predicted are immediately outdated due to observation latency ti​n​p​u​t−to​b​ssubscript𝑡𝑖𝑛𝑝𝑢𝑡subscript𝑡𝑜𝑏𝑠t_{input}-t_{obs}, policy inference latency to​u​t​p​u​t−ti​n​p​u​tsubscript𝑡𝑜𝑢𝑡𝑝𝑢𝑡subscript𝑡𝑖𝑛𝑝𝑢𝑡t_{output}-t_{input} and execution latency ta​c​t−to​u​t​p​u​tsubscript𝑡𝑎𝑐𝑡subscript𝑡𝑜𝑢𝑡𝑝𝑢𝑡t_{act}-t_{output}. We simply discard the outdated actions and only execute actions with the desired timestamp after ta​c​tsubscript𝑡𝑎𝑐𝑡t_{act} for each hardware.
items.zh: PD2. 相对末端执行器位姿。末端执行器位姿是UMI观测和动作空间的核心。为避免对实体/部署特定坐标的依赖，我们将所有末端执行器位姿表示为相对于夹爪当前末端执行器位姿。

items.en: PD2. Relative end-effector pose. End-effector (EE) pose is central to both UMI’s observation and action space. To avoid dependence on embodiment/deployment-specific coordinates, we represent all EE poses relative to gripper’s current EE pose.
items.zh: PD2.1) 相对末端执行器轨迹作为动作表示。先前工作已表明动作空间选择对任务性能有显著影响[9]，实验证据倾向于绝对位置动作而非增量动作。然而，我们发现相对轨迹表示（定义为从t_0开始的动作序列，即一系列SE(3)变换，表示t时刻相对于t_0时刻初始末端执行器位姿的期望位姿）使系统对数据收集期间的跟踪误差和相机位移更具鲁棒性。

items.en: PD2.1) Relative EE trajectory as action representation. Prior works have shown the significant impact of action space selection on task performance [9], with experimental evidence favoring absolute positional actions over delta actions. However, we found that a relative trajectory representation, defined for an action sequence starting at t0subscript𝑡0t_{0} as a sequence of S​E​(3)𝑆𝐸3SE(3) transforms denoting the desired pose at t𝑡t relative to the initial EE pose at t0subscript𝑡0t_{0}, allows the system to be more robust against tracking errors during data collection and camera displacements.
items.zh: PD2.2) 相对末端执行器轨迹作为本体感觉。类似地，我们将历史末端执行器位姿的本体感觉表示为相对轨迹。当观测历史长度设为2时，这种表示有效地向策略提供速度信息。结合我们的腕装相机观测空间，相对轨迹使系统无需标定。执行期间移动机器人基座不会影响任务性能（图10(a)），只要物体仍在可达范围内，这使得UMI框架也适用于移动操作器。

items.en: PD2.2) Relative EE trajectory as proprioception. Similarly, we represent the proprioception of history EE poses as a relative trajectory. When observation horizon is set to 2, this representation effectively provides velocity information to the policy. Combined with our wrist-mounted camera observation space, relative trajectory allows our system to be calibration-free. Moving the robot base during execution will not affect task performance (Fig. 10 (a)), as long as the objects are still within reach range, making the UMI framework applicable to mobile manipulators as well.
items.zh: 图6：相对轨迹作为动作表示。UMI使用的相对轨迹是每个推理步骤相对于同一当前末端执行器位姿的一系列末端执行器位姿。相比之下，增量动作将每个动作步骤相对于其直接前一个动作，因此会累积误差。绝对动作需要全局坐标系来定义所有动作，这对于野外数据收集难以定义。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Relative Trajectory as Action Representation. Relative trajectory, used by UMI, is a sequence of end-effector (EE) poses relative to the same current EE pose for each inference step. In contrast, Delta action represents each action step relative to its immediate previous action, therefore accumulates error. Absolute action requires a global coordinate frame for all actions, which is difficult to define for in-the-wild data collection.

items.en: PD2.3) Relative inter-gripper proprioception. When using UMI in a bimanual setup, we found that providing the policy with the relative pose between the two grippers to be critical for bimanual coordination and task success, as shown in Sec. V-C. The effect of inter-gripper proprioception is particularly large when the visual overlap between two cameras is small. The inter-gripper proprioception is enabled by our map-then-localize data collection scheme that constructs a scene-level global coordinate system HD4. For each new scene, we first collect a video that builds a map for the scene. Then, all demonstrations collected in this scene are relocalized to the same map, therefore sharing the same coordinate system. Despite the videos from each gripper being relocalized separately, the relative pose between two grippers at each time step can be calculated using their shared coordinates.
items.zh: PD2.3) 相对夹爪间本体感知。在双手设置中使用UMI时，我们发现为策略提供两个夹爪之间的相对位姿对于双手协调和任务成功至关重要，如第V-C节所示。当两个相机之间的视觉重叠较小时，夹爪间本体感知的效果尤其显著。夹爪间本体感知通过我们的“先建图后定位”数据收集方案实现，该方案构建了一个场景级全局坐标系HD4。对于每个新场景，我们首先收集一段视频来构建场景地图。然后，在该场景中收集的所有演示都被重新定位到同一张地图上，因此共享相同的坐标系。尽管每个夹爪的视频是分别重新定位的，但两个夹爪在每个时间步的相对位姿可以通过它们共享的坐标计算得出。

items.capEn: Figure 7: Policy Rollouts. We test UMI on a variety of challenging real-world tasks. Cup arrangement tests UMI’s ability to learn both prehensile and non-prehensile actions, and to capture multi-modal action distribution (clockwise and counter-clockwise rotation). This task is evaluated in both narrow-domain and unseen environments as well as two robot embodiments. Dynamic tossing tests UMI’s ability to capture and transfer rapid human motions as well as precise hand-eye coordination. Bimanual cloth folding tests UMI’s ability to synchronize two-arm coordination. Dish washing tests UMI’s ability to handle long-horizon tasks that involve multiple rigid, deformable, and articulated objects. Please check videos on our website for more details.
items.capZh: 图7：策略部署。我们在多种具有挑战性的真实世界任务上测试了UMI。杯子整理测试UMI学习抓取和非抓取动作以及捕捉多模态动作分布（顺时针和逆时针旋转）的能力。该任务在窄域和未见环境以及两种机器人本体上进行了评估。动态投掷测试UMI捕捉和传递快速人类运动以及精确手眼协调的能力。双手折叠布料测试UMI同步双臂协调的能力。洗碗测试UMI处理涉及多个刚性、可变形和铰接物体的长时域任务的能力。更多详情请查看我们网站上的视频。

items.en: In our experiment, we aim to evaluate the UMI framework’s effectiveness for deployable policy learning in three aspects:
items.zh: 在我们的实验中，我们旨在从三个方面评估UMI框架在可部署策略学习中的有效性：

items.en: Capability: How well can we transfer UMI demonstrations to effective robot policy? Especially for complex, dynamic, bimanual, and long-horizon manipulation skills.
items.zh: 能力：我们能否将UMI演示有效地迁移到机器人策略？特别是对于复杂、动态、双手和长时域的操作技能。

items.en: Generalization: Will data collected in the wild within diverse environments help the policy to generalize to unseen environments and objects?
items.zh: 泛化：在多样化环境中收集的野外数据是否有助于策略泛化到未见环境和物体？

items.en: Data collection efficiency: How fast can we collect manipulation data with UMI? What’s the accuracy of the SLAM system?
items.zh: 数据收集效率：使用UMI收集操作数据的速度有多快？SLAM系统的精度如何？

items.en: To access capability and generalization, we evaluate UMI on 4 real-world robotic tasks across both narrow domain and in-the-wild environments, shown in Fig. 7. To measure data collection efficiency, we compare the UMI gripper with human hand demonstration and a typical teleop interface. See §A.0-B for detailed data collection protocol.
items.zh: 为了评估能力和泛化，我们在4个真实世界机器人任务上评估了UMI，涵盖窄域和野外环境，如图7所示。为了衡量数据收集效率，我们将UMI夹爪与人类手部演示和典型的遥操作界面进行了比较。详细的数据收集协议见§A.0-B。

items.en: We study UMI’s ability to capture and transfer single-hand, bimanual, dynamic, and long-horizon manipulation skills with four tasks. For capability experiments, all tasks are evaluated in the same environment as data collection but with randomized robot and object initial states. To ensure a fair comparison, we use exactly the same initial state across all methods for both the robot and objects, by manually aligning the scene against pre-recorded images. See §A.0-C for detailed evaluation protocol and videos for all experiments.
items.zh: 我们通过四个任务研究了UMI捕捉和传递单手、双手、动态和长时域操作技能的能力。在能力实验中，所有任务在与数据收集相同的环境中进行评估，但机器人和物体的初始状态是随机化的。为了确保公平比较，我们通过手动将场景与预录图像对齐，使所有方法中机器人和物体的初始状态完全相同。详细的评估协议和所有实验的视频见§A.0-C。

items.capEn: Figure 8: Narrow-domain Evaluation Results. (a) Initial states for all evaluation episodes overlayed together. For each task, all methods start with the same set of initial states, matched manually with reference images. (b) Typical failure mode of the baseline/ablation policy. The red arrow indicates failure behavior, green arrow indicates desired behavior. (c) Success rate over 20 evaluation episodes, best performance for each column are bolded. Please check our website for more comparison videos.
items.capZh: 图8：窄域评估结果。(a) 所有评估回合的初始状态叠加在一起。对于每个任务，所有方法从相同的初始状态集合开始，通过参考图像手动匹配。(b) 基线/消融策略的典型失败模式。红色箭头表示失败行为，绿色箭头表示期望行为。(c) 20个评估回合的成功率，每列最佳性能以粗体显示。更多比较视频请查看我们的网站。

items.en: Task Place an espresso cup on the saucer with its handle facing to the left of the robot, Fig. 7. We defined task success as when the cup is placed upright on the saucer with its handle within ±15​°plus-or-minus15°\pm 15\degree to the left.
items.zh: 任务：将浓缩咖啡杯放在茶托上，杯柄朝向机器人左侧，图7。我们将任务成功定义为杯子直立放置在茶托上，杯柄在左侧±15°范围内。

items.en: Capability (what makes the task difficult?) This task tests the system’s ability to learn both prehensile (pick and place) and non-prehensile actions (i.e., pushing to reorientate the cup). When the handle faces straight away from the robot, the two equally valid solutions: rotation clockwise and counter-clockwise form a multi-modal action distribution. This task also tests UMI’s ability to sense relative depth through monocular camera observation and side mirrors.
items.zh: 能力（任务难点是什么？）：该任务测试系统学习抓取（拾取和放置）和非抓取动作（即推动以重新定向杯子）的能力。当杯柄正对机器人时，顺时针和逆时针旋转两种同样有效的解决方案形成了多模态动作分布。该任务还测试UMI通过单目相机观察和侧镜感知相对深度的能力。

items.en: Performance The training dataset contains 305 episodes collected by 2 demonstrators, evaluation includes 20 test cases, with the testing initial state distribution shown in Fig. 8 (a). UMI can complete the task 20/20. The next paragraphs will discuss our ablation studies around our key design decisions.
items.zh: 性能：训练数据集包含由2名演示者收集的305个回合，评估包括20个测试案例，测试初始状态分布如图8(a)所示。UMI可以20/20完成该任务。以下段落将讨论围绕我们关键设计决策的消融研究。

items.en: Cross-robot generalization: To demonstrate UMI’s cross-embodiment generality, we also deployed the same policy checkpoint on a Franka Emika FR2 robot, shown in Fig. 1 and Fig. 8. This experiment achieves 18/20=90%1820percent9018/20=90\% success rate, with the 2 failure cases being joint limit violations, which could have been avoided if we had mounted the FR2 robot at a different location.
items.zh: 跨机器人泛化：为了展示UMI的跨本体通用性，我们还在Franka Emika FR2机器人上部署了相同的策略检查点，如图1和图8所示。该实验实现了18/20=90%的成功率，2个失败案例是由于关节极限限制，如果我们将FR2机器人安装在不同位置，本可以避免。

items.en: No Fisheye lens [HD2]: To ablate the importance of having a wide field-of-view (FoV) Fisheye lens, we post-processed the dataset by rectifying and cropping each image to a square with 69​°69°69\degree horizontal and vertical FoV. This is a generous analogy of RealSense D415 (69​°69°69\degree HFoV, 42​°42°42\degree VFoV) and iPhone wide camera (69​°69°69\degree HFoV, 51​°51°51\degree VFoV). This baseline only achieves 11/20=55%1120percent5511/20=55\% success rate. Beyond the expected failure mode where the cup is outside of camera view, we found this baseline policy to perform surprisingly poor even if the object is visible, with often jittery motions. We suspect that during training, the poor object visibility forced the policy to be unnecessarily multimodal.
items.zh: 无鱼眼镜头[HD2]：为了消融宽视场（FoV）鱼眼镜头的重要性，我们通过校正和裁剪每张图像为水平和垂直FoV均为69°的正方形来后处理数据集。这是RealSense D415（69° HFoV，42° VFoV）和iPhone广角相机（69° HFoV，51° VFoV）的慷慨类比。该基线仅达到11/20=55%的成功率。除了杯子在相机视野外的预期失败模式外，我们发现即使物体可见，该基线策略的表现也出奇地差，经常出现抖动运动。我们怀疑在训练过程中，较差的物体可见性迫使策略变得不必要地多模态。

items.en: Alternative action spaces [PD2]: As alternatives to our relative trajectory as action representation, we also consider absolute and delta action spaces as illustrated in Fig 6. Since the SLAM system outputs pose relative to the first frame of the mapping video (details in §A.0-D), we can only calculate relative and delta actions directly using SLAM output. To compute absolute actions in the robot base frame, we calibrate both SLAM coordinates and the robot with respect to the same fiducial markers [16] placed on the table.
items.zh: 替代动作空间[PD2]：作为我们相对轨迹动作表示的替代方案，我们还考虑了绝对动作空间和增量动作空间，如图6所示。由于SLAM系统输出相对于建图视频第一帧的位姿（详见§A.0-D），我们只能直接使用SLAM输出计算相对动作和增量动作。为了计算机器人基坐标系中的绝对动作，我们将SLAM坐标和机器人相对于放置在桌子上的相同基准标记[16]进行校准。

items.en: The delta action baseline achieves 16/20=80%1620percent8016/20=80\% success rate. The absolute action baseline performs surprisingly poorly with only 5/20=25%520percent255/20=25\% success rate, demonstrating a noticeable bias in action selection, likely due to inaccurate calibration between the SLAM and robot base coordinate frames (Fig. 8 (b)). While theoretically the performance of this baseline could approach that of relative trajectory with better calibration, this experiment underscores the difficulty of obtaining action data with absolute coordinates, even in controlled lab settings.
items.zh: 增量动作基线达到16/20=80%的成功率。绝对动作基线表现得出奇地差，仅有5/20=25%的成功率，显示出明显的动作选择偏差，可能是由于SLAM和机器人基坐标系之间的校准不准确（图8(b)）。虽然理论上该基线的性能可以通过更好的校准接近相对轨迹，但该实验强调了即使在受控实验室环境中获取绝对坐标动作数据的难度。

items.en: Effect of side mirrors [HD3]: To our surprise, directly providing mirror images decreases the performance from 18/20=90%1820percent9018/20=90\% (no mirror) to 17/20=85%1720percent8517/20=85\%. To fully take advantage of side mirrors, we need to digitally reflect the content inside mirrors and swap left and right mirror images, which achieves a 20/20=100%2020percent10020/20=100\% success rate. We hypothesize that without digital reflection, the opposite motions observed in the main and mirrored images might confuse vision encoders, especially those with translational equivariance.
items.zh: 侧镜效果[HD3]：令我们惊讶的是，直接提供镜像图像将性能从18/20=90%（无镜子）降低到17/20=85%。为了充分利用侧镜，我们需要对镜子内的内容进行数字反射并交换左右镜像图像，这实现了20/20=100%的成功率。我们假设，如果没有数字反射，主图像和镜像图像中观察到的相反运动可能会混淆视觉编码器，尤其是那些具有平移等变性的编码器。

items.capEn: Figure 9: In-the-wild Cup Arrangement Evaluation. (a) The policy, trained with in-the-wild data, demonstrates strong generalization capability to novel environments and novel objects. (b) With only narrow-domain data, the policy struggles to generalize to new environments. (c) Comprehensive qualitative results are provided, and additional comparison videos can be found on our website.
items.capZh: 图9：野外杯子整理评估。(a) 使用野外数据训练的策略展示了强大的泛化能力，能够适应新环境和新物体。(b) 仅使用窄域数据，策略难以泛化到新环境。(c) 提供了全面的定性结果，更多比较视频可在我们的网站上找到。

items.en: Task The robot is tasked to sort 6 objects from the YCB object set [5] randomly placed on a table by tossing them to the corresponding bin. The 3 spherical objects (baseball, orange, apple) should be tossed into the round bin, while the 3 Lego Duplo pieces go into the rectangular bin (Fig. 7). The bins are placed beyond the robot’s kinematic reach range to highlight the necessity of dynamic action for this task.
items.zh: 任务：机器人被要求将YCB物体集[5]中随机放置在桌子上的6个物体分类，通过将它们投掷到相应的箱子中。3个球形物体（棒球、橙子、苹果）应投掷到圆形箱子中，而3个乐高得宝积木应投掷到矩形箱子中（图7）。箱子放置在机器人运动范围之外，以突出该任务中动态动作的必要性。

items.en: Capability: The dynamic tossing task demonstrates UMI’s ability to capture and transfer fluid and rapid human motions, precise hand-eye coordination (between RGB and proprioception) and timing alignment (between robot and gripper).
items.zh: 能力：动态投掷任务展示了UMI捕捉和传递流畅快速的人类运动、精确的手眼协调（RGB和本体感知之间）以及时间对齐（机器人和夹爪之间）的能力。

items.en: Performance: We collected 280 demonstration episodes for this task, with mixed multi and single-object picking and tossing. Our policy (with inference time latency matching) achieves 105/120=87.5%105120percent87.5105/120=87.5\% success rate, counted by the number of objects successfully tossed to their corresponding bin.
items.zh: 性能：我们为此任务收集了280个示范片段，包含混合的多物体和单物体抓取与投掷。我们的策略（在推理时间延迟匹配下）实现了105/120=87.5%的成功率，按成功投掷到对应箱子的物体数量计算。

items.en: No Latency Matching [PD1]: With the same trained policy, we disable inference-time latency matching by setting the measured latencies for all observation and action streams to 0. We visually observe the policy’s movement is much more jittery due to the out-of-sync observations and executions.
items.zh: 无延迟匹配[PD1]：使用相同的训练策略，我们通过将所有观测和动作流的测量延迟设置为0来禁用推理时延迟匹配。我们观察到，由于观测和执行不同步，策略的运动明显更加抖动。

items.en: While the jitteriness minimally affects grasping, its impact on tossing performance is notable as it disrupts the robot motion to achieve the desired tossing velocity, as illustrated in the elbow joint velocity curve in Fig. 8. In addition, the misalignment between the gripper and robot action (due to their different execution latency) leads to suboptimal object release during tossing. As a result, the final success rate decreased to 69/120=57.5%69120percent57.569/120=57.5\%.
items.zh: 虽然抖动对抓取影响较小，但对投掷性能的影响显著，因为它破坏了机器人达到所需投掷速度的运动，如图8中的肘关节速度曲线所示。此外，夹爪与机器人动作之间的错位（由于执行延迟不同）导致投掷时物体释放不理想。因此，最终成功率降至69/120=57.5%。

items.en: Task Two robot arms need to coordinate and fold the sweater’s sleeves inward, fold up the bottom hem, rotate 90 degrees, and finally fold the sweater in half again (Fig. 7). See §A.0-C for detailed evaluation protocol.
items.zh: 任务：两个机器人手臂需要协调将毛衣袖子向内折叠，向上折叠下摆，旋转90度，最后再次对折毛衣（图7）。详细评估协议见§A.0-C。

items.en: Capability Manipulating high degrees of freedom deformable objects is challenging due to their complex dynamics and underactuation. In addition, this task requires tight coordination between arms. For example, lifting the bottom hem requires two arms to pick it up at the same time, and if one of the arm is just slightly too fast or slow this step will fail.
items.zh: 能力：操作高自由度可变形物体因其复杂的动力学和欠驱动性而具有挑战性。此外，该任务需要手臂之间的紧密协调。例如，抬起下摆需要两只手臂同时拾取，如果其中一只手臂稍快或稍慢，这一步就会失败。

items.en: Performance We collected 250 demonstrations from two demonstrators, with randomized initial states by translating/rotating the shirt and folding the sleeves. We use a single centralized policy to generate actions for both robot arms and grippers. Our policy achieves 14/20=70%1420percent7014/20=70\% success rate.
items.zh: 性能：我们从两位演示者处收集了250个示范片段，初始状态通过平移/旋转衬衫和折叠袖子随机化。我们使用单个集中式策略为两个机器人手臂和夹爪生成动作。我们的策略实现了14/20=70%的成功率。

items.en: No relative inter-gripper proprioception [PD2.3]: Without inter-gripper proprioception information (during both training and eval), the coordination between the two arms becomes significantly worse. The most salient failure case is when the two arms lift the bottom hem of the shirt, where the baseline policy often misses one of the grasps due to asynchronous grasp action (Fig. 8 (b)). As a result, the baseline policy only achieves success rate of 6/20=30%620percent306/20=30\%. In contrast, UMI policy synchronizes the grasp by first reaching the pre-grasp pose and waiting until both arms are in position before simultaneously grasping and folding.
items.zh: 无相对夹爪间本体感觉[PD2.3]：没有夹爪间本体感觉信息（在训练和评估期间），两臂之间的协调显著变差。最显著的失败案例是当两只手臂抬起衬衫下摆时，基线策略由于异步抓取动作常常错过其中一个抓取（图8(b)）。因此，基线策略仅达到6/20=30%的成功率。相比之下，UMI策略通过首先到达预抓取姿态并等待两只手臂到位，然后同时抓取和折叠来同步抓取。

items.capEn: Figure 10: Robustness Test. We test the policy robustness with different inference time perturbations such as moving robot base, novel objects, different lighting conditions, and adding different and more condiments for the dish-washing task. The learned policy is robust against these perturbations and completes the task. Please check the video on our website for more details.
items.capZh: 图10：鲁棒性测试。我们测试了策略在不同推理时间扰动下的鲁棒性，例如移动机器人基座、新物体、不同光照条件，以及为洗碗任务添加不同和更多的调味品。学习到的策略对这些扰动具有鲁棒性并完成任务。更多详情请查看我们网站上的视频。

items.en: Task The robot needs to execute 7 steps of sequentially dependent actions (turn on faucet, grasp plate, pick up sponge, wash and wipe plate until ketchups are removed, place plate, place the sponge and turn off faucet), shown in Fig. 7. See §A.0-C for detailed evaluation protocol.
items.zh: 任务：机器人需要执行7个顺序依赖的动作步骤（打开水龙头、抓取盘子、拿起海绵、清洗和擦拭盘子直到番茄酱被去除、放置盘子、放置海绵并关闭水龙头），如图7所示。详细评估协议见§A.0-C。

items.en: Capability This task pushes the boundaries of robot manipulation capability from several fronts: 1) it is an ultra-long horizon task where each step’s success depends on the previous one; 2) the robot needs to perceive and manipulate complex fluid including both Newtonian fluid (i.e., water) and non-Newtonian fluid (i.e., ketchup). 3) the wiping motion requires using a deformable tool (i.e., sponge) while coordinating both arms with reference to the water stream; 4) manipulating constrained articulated object (i.e., turning on and off faucet) requires mechanical compliance provided by soft fingers; 5) the policy also need to be semantically robust to the concept of “cleanliness”. When additional ketchup is added during washing or even after the washing phase is done, the robot needs to resume washing and wiping.
items.zh: 能力：该任务从多个方面推动了机器人操作能力的边界：1）这是一个超长时域任务，每一步的成功依赖于前一步；2）机器人需要感知和操作复杂流体，包括牛顿流体（即水）和非牛顿流体（即番茄酱）；3）擦拭动作需要使用可变形工具（即海绵），同时协调双臂并参考水流；4）操作受限的铰接物体（即打开和关闭水龙头）需要软手指提供的机械顺应性；5）策略还需要在语义上对“清洁”概念具有鲁棒性。当在洗涤过程中或洗涤阶段完成后添加额外的番茄酱时，机器人需要恢复洗涤和擦拭。

items.en: Performance A single demonstrator collected 258 demonstrations with randomized initial states including, ketchup patterns, position of the plate and sponge, along with water faucet angle. The collected demo also include explicit demonstrations of recovery behavior when additional ketchup is added. For this task, we train diffusion policy by fine-tuning a CLIP [29] pretrained ViT-B/16 [11] vision encoder. Overall, UMI achieves 14/20=70%1420percent7014/20=70\% success rate. In addition, we demonstrate the robustness of our policy against various distractors, and types of sauce (mustard, chocolate syrup, caramel syrup), as well as robustness against perturbations, see Fig. 10 and video on our website for details.
items.zh: 性能：一位演示者收集了258个示范片段，初始状态随机化，包括番茄酱图案、盘子和海绵的位置以及水龙头角度。收集的示范还包括当添加额外番茄酱时的恢复行为的明确示范。对于此任务，我们通过微调CLIP [29]预训练的ViT-B/16 [11]视觉编码器来训练扩散策略。总体而言，UMI实现了14/20=70%的成功率。此外，我们展示了策略对各种干扰物和酱料类型（芥末、巧克力糖浆、焦糖糖浆）的鲁棒性，以及对扰动的鲁棒性，详情见图10和网站上的视频。

items.en: No CLIP-pretrained ViT vision encoder. For this visually complex task, we found training ResNet-34 [17] from scratch to be insufficient. Specifically, the baseline policy with ResNet-34 learned an non-reactive behavior and ignored any variation in plate or sponge position. As a result, it cannot perform the task, 0/10=0%010percent00/10=0\%.
items.zh: 无CLIP预训练的ViT视觉编码器：对于这个视觉复杂的任务，我们发现从头训练ResNet-34 [17]是不够的。具体来说，使用ResNet-34的基线策略学习了非反应性行为，忽略了盘子或海绵位置的任何变化。因此，它无法执行任务，成功率为0/10=0%。

items.en: Prior works in behavior cloning typically only evaluate in the same environment as data collection, often limited by their inability to collect sufficiently diverse dataset to allow generalization. By not relying on teleoperation with real robots, UMI enables low-cost data collection in any environment, which we refer to as in-the-wild data.
items.zh: 先前在行为克隆方面的工作通常只在数据收集的相同环境中进行评估，通常受限于无法收集足够多样化的数据集以实现泛化。通过不依赖真实机器人的遥操作，UMI实现了在任何环境中的低成本数据收集，我们称之为野外数据。

items.en: We evaluate UMI’s ability to produce generalizable visuomotor policies by scaling up the cup arrangement task Sec. V-A to novel environments and novel objects. Within 12 person-hours, 3 demonstrators collected 1400 demonstrations for the cup arrangement task across 30 diverse physical locations, including homes, offices, restaurants, and outdoor environments. The demonstrations involved 15 espresso cups of different colors, shapes (cylindrical and tapered), and materials (ceramic, glass, and metal). To ensure model capacity, we increased the vision encoder further to CLIP [30] pretrained ViT-L/14 [11]. We evaluated our UMI policy in two unseen environments (Fig. 9 first column):
items.zh: 我们通过将杯子排列任务（第V-A节）扩展到新环境和新物体来评估UMI生成可泛化视觉运动策略的能力。在12人时内，3位演示者在30个不同的物理位置（包括家庭、办公室、餐厅和室外环境）收集了1400个杯子排列任务的示范片段。示范涉及15个不同颜色、形状（圆柱形和锥形）和材料（陶瓷、玻璃和金属）的浓缩咖啡杯。为确保模型容量，我们将视觉编码器进一步升级为CLIP [30]预训练的ViT-L/14 [11]。我们在两个未见过的环境中评估了UMI策略（图9第一列）：

items.en: Cafe table is a metal table in the outdoor seating area of a busy cafe where a large number of pedestrians serve as natural distractors. We tested 5 cups in the training set and 2 testing (unseen) cups, with 5 initial poses each, 35 experiments in total.
items.zh: 咖啡桌：繁忙咖啡馆户外座位区的金属桌，大量行人作为自然干扰物。我们测试了训练集中的5个杯子和2个测试（未见过的）杯子，每个杯子5个初始姿态，共35个实验。

items.en: Water fountain is a black cubic water fountain, with a thin film of water constantly flowing from the center, covering the entire top surface. This environment is notably out-of-distribution since all of our demonstrations are collected on non-black tables, not to mention changes in surface dynamics due to the presence of water. We tested 3 training and 2 testing cups, with 5 initial poses each, 25 experiments in total.
items.zh: 喷泉：黑色立方体喷泉，中心不断有薄水流过，覆盖整个顶部表面。这个环境明显是分布外的，因为所有示范都是在非黑色桌子上收集的，更不用说由于水的存在导致的表面动力学变化。我们测试了3个训练杯子和2个测试杯子，每个杯子5个初始姿态，共25个实验。

items.en: We selected the 2 testing cups such that one has an out-of-distribution color (dark blue), while the other has an unseen texture (brown rings). For each test case, we vary the initial pose of both the cup and the saucer.
items.zh: 我们选择了2个测试杯子，其中一个具有分布外的颜色（深蓝色），另一个具有未见过的纹理（棕色环）。对于每个测试案例，我们改变杯子和碟子的初始姿态。

items.en: Our UMI policy has 28/40=70%2840percent7028/40=70\% success rate on training cups and 15/20=75%1520percent7515/20=75\% success rate on testing cups, with a combined success rate of 43/60=71.7%4360percent71.743/60=71.7\%. More qualitative and quantitative results are shown in Fig. 9.
items.zh: 我们的UMI策略在训练杯子上有28/40=70%的成功率，在测试杯子上有15/20=75%的成功率，综合成功率为43/60=71.7%。更多定性和定量结果如图9所示。

items.en: No in-the-wild data. To validate the generalization ability comes from in-the-wild data, instead of pretrained vision backbone, we trained another model that only uses data from our narrow-domain experiment collected in the same lab environment (described in Sec. V-A) with the same pretrained ViT vision backbone. In the same unseen environments, as shown in Fig. 9(b), the robot with the baseline policy doesn’t even move toward the cup. As a result, its success rate is 0%.
items.zh: 无野外数据：为了验证泛化能力来自野外数据而非预训练视觉骨干，我们训练了另一个模型，仅使用在同一实验室环境中收集的窄域实验数据（第V-A节描述），并使用相同的预训练ViT视觉骨干。在相同的未见环境中，如图9(b)所示，使用基线策略的机器人甚至不向杯子移动。因此，其成功率为0%。

items.en: Takeaway. This result indicates that finetuning a large pre-trained model with narrow-domain data is insufficient for producing an in-the-wild deployable policy. Therefore collecting diverse, in-the-wild data is still critical for effective generalization to novel environments and objects.
items.zh: 结论：这一结果表明，仅使用窄域数据微调大型预训练模型不足以产生可在野外部署的策略。因此，收集多样化的野外数据对于有效泛化到新环境和新物体仍然至关重要。

items.capEn: Figure 11: Data Collection Throughput Comparison among UMI, bare hand, and teleoperation with a space mouse. UMI is not only faster than traditional teleoperation but also able to perform tasks that were not possible with typical teleoperation interfaces (e.g., dynamic tossing).
items.capZh: 图 11: UMI、裸手和空间鼠标遥操作的数据采集吞吐量比较。UMI 不仅比传统遥操作更快，还能执行典型遥操作界面无法完成的任务（例如动态抛掷）。

items.capEn: Figure 12: SLAM Accuracy. We evaluate the SLAM accuracy with a MoCap benchmark including 7 single-gripper tasks and 7 bimanual tasks. Overall, we can achieve less than 1 cm and 4°°\degree tracking error.
items.capZh: 图 12: SLAM 精度。我们使用包含 7 个单夹爪任务和 7 个双臂任务的 MoCap 基准评估 SLAM 精度。总体而言，我们能够实现小于 1 cm 和 4° 的跟踪误差。

full.sec: VII Data Collection Throughput and Accuracy
full.secZh: 七、数据采集吞吐量与精度

items.en: Throughput. UMI’s improved ergonomics and intuitiveness over teleoperations also lead to improved data collection throughput. To demonstrate this effect, we record the number of demonstrations that can be collected within 15 minutes by the same operation using 3 different methods (Fig. 11): 1) Human hand demonstration 2) UMI gripper 3) Spacemouse-based teleoperation, which is a typical teleoperation interface used in many learning from demonstration works [9, 54]. We measure the data throughput on two tasks: 1) cup arrangement 2) dynamic tossing. Note that the time taken to reset the environment, randomize objects, and handle robot faults (such as self-collisions) are also counted in this experiment to accurately represent the real-world data collection throughput.
items.zh: 吞吐量。UMI 相比遥操作在人体工程学和直观性上的改进也提高了数据采集吞吐量。为展示这一效果，我们记录了同一操作者使用 3 种不同方法在 15 分钟内可采集的演示次数（图 11）：1) 人手演示 2) UMI 夹爪 3) 基于空间鼠标的遥操作，这是许多模仿学习工作中使用的典型遥操作界面 [9, 54]。我们在两个任务上测量数据吞吐量：1) 杯子摆放 2) 动态抛掷。注意，环境重置、物体随机化和处理机器人故障（如自碰撞）的时间也计入本实验，以准确反映真实世界的数据采集吞吐量。

items.en: On the cup arrangement task, the UMI gripper is more than 3×3\times faster than teleportation, at 48%percent4848\% speed of the human hand, shown in Fig. 11 (d). Note that human is significantly faster on reset and randomization, due to their proximity to the objects. On the dynamic tossing task, the UMI gripper is at 64%percent6464\% speed of the human hand, while the teleportation method failed to produce a single successful demonstration in 15 minutes.
items.zh: 在杯子摆放任务上，UMI 夹爪比遥操作快 3 倍以上，达到人手速度的 48%，如图 11(d) 所示。注意，由于人手更接近物体，在重置和随机化方面明显更快。在动态抛掷任务上，UMI 夹爪达到人手速度的 64%，而遥操作方法在 15 分钟内未能产生一次成功演示。

items.en: Accuracy. To independently assess the accuracy of our SLAM-based tracking system, we collected a SLAM benchmark dataset with MoCap ground truth. The dataset contains 7 single-gripper tasks and 7 bimanual tasks, all with a variety of movable objects in view as well as natural and rapid human motion. As shown in Fig. 12, our SLAM system has a mean Absolute Trajectory Error (ATE) of 6.1​m​m6.1𝑚𝑚6.1mm for position and 3.5​°3.5°3.5\degree for rotation. Since both grippers are localized with the same map, we can also obtain the relative pose between two grippers (i.e. inter-gripper pose PD2.3). The mean Relative Pose Error (RPE) between two grippers is 10.1​m​m10.1𝑚𝑚10.1mm for position and 0.8​°0.8°0.8\degree for rotation.
items.zh: 精度。为独立评估我们基于 SLAM 的跟踪系统的精度，我们收集了一个带有 MoCap 真值的 SLAM 基准数据集。该数据集包含 7 个单夹爪任务和 7 个双臂任务，所有任务都有视野中的各种可移动物体以及自然快速的人体运动。如图 12 所示，我们的 SLAM 系统的平均绝对轨迹误差（ATE）位置为 6.1 mm，旋转为 3.5°。由于两个夹爪通过同一地图定位，我们还可以获得两个夹爪之间的相对位姿（即夹爪间位姿 PD2.3）。两个夹爪之间的平均相对位姿误差（RPE）位置为 10.1 mm，旋转为 0.8°。

items.en: While UMI demonstrates policy efficacy across a wide range of tasks and scenarios, a few limitations remain. First, since the kinematics limits of the downstream deployment robots are unknown at the time of data collection, we rely on data filtering to ensure the kinematic feasibility of the resulting policy. Future works could develop an embodiment-aware policy learning framework that can transfer skills from valid but hardware-infeasible actions.
items.zh: 尽管 UMI 在广泛的任务和场景中展示了策略有效性，但仍存在一些局限性。首先，由于数据采集时下游部署机器人的运动学限制未知，我们依赖数据过滤来确保所得策略的运动学可行性。未来工作可以开发一种具身感知的策略学习框架，能够从有效但硬件不可行的动作中迁移技能。

items.en: Second, our SLAM-based action recovery system inherits visual SLAM’s requirement for sufficient texture in the environment. Future works could leverage static third-person-view cameras, coupled with additional fiducial markers on UMI grippers to recover action even in texture-deficient environments like rooms with pure white walls.
items.zh: 其次，我们基于 SLAM 的动作恢复系统继承了视觉 SLAM 对环境足够纹理的要求。未来工作可以利用静态的第三人称视角相机，结合 UMI 夹爪上的额外基准标记，即使在纹理缺乏的环境（如纯白墙壁的房间）中也能恢复动作。

items.en: Third, collecting data with UMI grippers is still less efficient than human hand demonstration, as shown in Sec. VII. This is in part due to the gripper’s weight and bulkiness, and in part due to the reduced degrees of freedom compared to human hands. Future works could explore lighter materials and further improve UMI gripper’s mechanical design and ergonomics, or alternatively, build sufficiently capable dexterous robotic hands and policies that can directly transfer from human motions.
items.zh: 第三，如第七节所示，使用 UMI 夹爪采集数据仍然比人手演示效率低。这部分是由于夹爪的重量和体积，部分是由于与人手相比自由度减少。未来工作可以探索更轻的材料，进一步改进 UMI 夹爪的机械设计和人体工程学，或者构建足够灵巧的机器人手和能够直接从人体运动迁移的策略。

items.en: We present Universal Manipulation Interface (UMI), a framework that enables learning capable and generalizable manipulation policies directly from in-the-wild human demonstrations. The UMI gripper, a hand-held demonstration interface, captures sufficient information to learn some challenging manipulation tasks, including washing a dirty dish, bimanual sweater folding, and dynamic object tossing and sorting. At the same time, UMI remains highly scalable for in-the-wild data collection with its portability, cost-effectiveness, and operational simplicity. By recording all information in a single, standardized MP4 file, UMI’s data can be easily shared over the Internet, allowing geographically distributed data collection from a large pool of nonexpert demonstrators. Our goal with UMI is to democratize robotic data collection, fostering a vast, diverse, and decentralized dataset to emerge from the robotics community
items.zh: 我们提出了通用操作接口（UMI），这是一个框架，能够直接从野外人类演示中学习有能力和可泛化的操作策略。UMI 夹爪是一种手持式演示接口，捕获了足够的信息来学习一些具有挑战性的操作任务，包括清洗脏盘子、双臂折叠毛衣以及动态物体抛掷和分拣。同时，UMI 凭借其便携性、成本效益和操作简便性，在野外数据采集方面保持高度可扩展性。通过将所有信息记录在单个标准化的 MP4 文件中，UMI 的数据可以轻松通过互联网共享，允许来自大量非专家演示者的地理分布式数据采集。我们的目标是利用 UMI 实现机器人数据采集的民主化，促进从机器人社区涌现出庞大、多样且去中心化的数据集。

items.zh: 这项工作部分得到了丰田研究所、NSF 奖项 #2037101 和 #2132519 的支持。我们感谢 Google 和 TRI 提供的 UR5 机器人，以及 IRIS 和 IPRL 实验室提供的 Franka 机器人硬件。本文包含的观点和结论仅代表作者本人，不应被解释为必然代表赞助商的官方政策（明示或暗示）。

items.en: We would like to thank Andy Zeng, Pete Florance, Huy Ha, Yihuai Gao, Samir Gadre, Mandi Zhao, Mengda Xu, Alper Canberk, Kevin Zakka, Zeyi Liu, Dominik Bauer, Tony Zhao, Zipeng Fu and Lucy Shi for their thoughtful discussions. We thank Alex Alspach, Brandan Hathaway, Aimee Goncalves, Phoebe Horgan, and Jarod Wilson for their help on hardware design and prototyping. We thank Naveen Kuppuswamy, Dale McConachie, and Calder Phillips-Graffine for their help on low-level controllers. We thank John Lenard, Frank Michel, Charles Richter, and Xiang Li for their advice on SLAM. We thank Eric Dusel, Nwabisi C., and Letica Priebe Rocha for their help on the MoCap dataset collection. We thank Chen Wang, Zhou Xian, Moo Jin Kim, and Marion Lepert for their assistance with the Franka setup. We especially thank Steffen Urban for his open-source projects on GoPro SLAM and Camera-IMU calibration, and John @ 3D printing world for inspiration of the gripper mechanism.
